analog_clock.summary: Analog clock (`analog_clock`) is useful application, it has analog clock, calendar and kicken timer.

cd.summary: Change working directory. Note this is global value, shared between shells and applications. The applicaitons (such as pem editor) do not know the change.

clip.summary: a simple application to save the content of clipboard to file.

cmd.summary: launch a new command shell. Syntax is 'cmd [screen_number].

cp.summary: Copy file(s). Supports wildcards in src. `-r` for recursive copy. dst can be a directory when copying multiple files.

curl.summary: HTTP client for simple web requests. Supports `http://` and `https://`, `-L` to follow redirects (up to 5), `-m SECONDS` request timeout, `-I` HEAD request (status + headers only), `-o FILE` to save body to file, `-O` to save under the URL's filename, `-X METHOD` to choose request method, `-d DATA` to send request body (`-d @file` reads it from a file), `-A UA` to set the User-Agent, `-u user:password` for HTTP basic auth, `-i` to include response headers, `-s` for silent mode, and `-V` to show version. `-H` for header. The URL goes last.

dic.summary: `dic` looks up the meaning of a word using ChatGPT.

diff.summary: Compare two text files. Supports unified and side-by-side views, paging, output to file, and configurable context lines.

docs.summary: an AI help agennt for Pocket Deck.

flashcards.summary: a flash card app to learn words.

gpt.summary: gpt (`gpt`) is the AI assistant frontend.

gpt_rt.summary: a real-time voice agent using the OpenAI Realtime API.

graph.summary: Graph ('graph') gives Obsidian-style Markdown graph view.

grep.summary: Search text in files, Linux-like. The pattern is a **regex by default** (MicroPython's limited `re`; unsupported patterns fall back to literal match). `-F` literal/fixed-string match, `-v` invert match, `-r` recursive, `-n` line numbers, `-i` ignore case, `-l` filenames only, `-A/-B/-C N` context lines, `-c` count only, `-m N` stop after N matches, `--no-filename` hide the filename prefix, `--include .py,.md` filter by extension, `--max N` skip files larger than N bytes. (`-e`/`-E` accepted but redundant since regex is the default.)

head.summary: Print first lines of file(s). Default N is 10.

home.summary: Home app (`home`) is an app to launch apps and change settings.

journal.summary: Journal (`journal`) analizes journal Markdown file and visualizes to chart.

listup.summary: `listup` command adds a file to filelist for netserver sharing (Web and iOS app).

lock.summary: Lock the device. `lock 0912` sets the PIN to `0912` and locks; `lock` locks using the already-stored PIN. See "Locking the device" below.

ls.summary: List files. Supports wildcards (e.g. `ls *.py`). `-l` for detailed view with size and date. `-R` for recursive search, '-r' for reverse order. `-c N` to copy filename at index N to clipboard (-1 for last).

netserver.summary: launch network server to serve services. It provide screencast and clipboard sharing. See [[netserver/GETTING_STARTED]] for detail.

pem.summary: Pem (`pem`) is emacs-inspired powerful editor written by Python.

qr.summary: `qr` generates a QR Code from text or URL inputs and displays it centered on the screen.

recorder.summary: `recorder` records audio to a WAV file.

scp.summary: scp command. Syntax is "scp host username password copy_from copy_to". Remote path starts with ":". Example: scp 192.168.1.100 user password test.txt :/home/user/test.txt  .

setjpf.summary: Change terminal font to Japanese. It's lighter than setuni.

setuni.summary: Change terminal font to CJK Unicode font,.

ssh.summary: ssh command. Syntax is "ssh host username password".

stt.summary: a Speech-to-Text app using OpenAI Whisper API.

sync.summary: a bidirectional file sync tool that keeps folders on Pocket Deck in sync with a remote machine over SSH.

tail.summary: Print last lines of file(s). Default N is 10.

voicerecorder.summary: `voicerecorder` records mono audio optimized for voice (low sample rate, small file size).

wavfileplay.summary: `wavfileplay` plays a single WAV file directly.

wavplay.summary: Wavplay (`wavplay`) is an audio player, CLI version of music.
